Title: Isolation and Identification of miRNAs in Jatropha curcas

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major component influencing oil quality for biofuel use
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf (tested by VIGS/GC); fruit and seed discussed as oil-related organs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing primary JcumiR004 reduced linoleic acid (C18:2) to undetectable levels in systemic leaves and increased expression of four target genes.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JcumiR004 was identified as a jatropha miRNA and showed relatively high expression in developing fruit and high expression in seed by qRT-PCR. 2. VIGS silencing of primary JcumiR004 decreased JcumiR004 expression and significantly upregulated four predicted targets: UBC22, Os01g0857700/BUD31 homolog 1, PAG1, and ARF7. 3. Silencing JcumiR004 significantly altered fatty acid composition, specifically lowering linoleic acid (C18:2) in leaves, indicating a role in oil composition.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous in root, stem, leaf, developing fruit and mature seed; relatively high in developing fruit; also highly expressed in seed
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitous in root, stem, leaf, developing fruit and mature seed; relatively high in developing fruit; also highly expressed in seed
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: small RNA cloning and sequencing; qRT-PCR expression profiling; northern blot; 3' and 5' RACE precursor isolation; VIGS silencing of primary miRNA; target gene expression analysis by qRT-PCR; fatty acid profiling by gas chromatography
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: small RNA cloning/sequencing from leaf and seed libraries; target prediction from jatropha cDNA library; metabolite profiling by GC
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing fruit versus mature seed; TRV-VIGS treatment in leaves; greenhouse/growth chamber conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering jatropha oil composition, especially linoleic acid proportion, but causal downstream metabolic genes remain unresolved so deployment requires caution.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The metabolic effect was demonstrated in leaves using TRV-VIGS, not directly in seed oil. The study states JcumiR004 modulates oil composition but does not identify the direct fatty-acid-pathway gene regulated downstream.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: VIGS caused photobleaching and leaf spotting phenotypes, suggesting pleiotropic effects on growth/development.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major plant oil component; determinant of jatropha oil quality/biofuel trait
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf in validation assay; fruit and seed are oil-relevant organs discussed in the study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator whose silencing decreases linoleic acid (C18:2) and derepresses four target genes.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JcumiR004 is a conserved jatropha miRNA with abundant mature and precursor forms in developing fruit. 2. Silencing primary JcumiR004 via TRV-VIGS significantly increased transcript abundance of four predicted targets: UBC22, Os01g0857700/BUD31 homolog 1, PAG1, and ARF7. 3. JcumiR004 silencing caused a marked reduction of linoleic acid (C18:2), linking this miRNA to fatty acid composition control.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: UBC22; Os01g0857700 (Protein BUD31 homolog 1); PAG1; ARF7
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses UBC22, Os01g0857700/BUD31 homolog 1, PAG1, and ARF7; silencing JcumiR004 upregulated these targets by 41.5-, 6.5-, 93.4-, and 18.5-fold, respectively
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: fatty acid composition regulation; oil trait modulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous in root, stem, leaf, developing fruit and mature seed; relatively high in developing fruit; highly expressed in seed
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitous in root, stem, leaf, developing fruit and mature seed; relatively high in developing fruit; highly expressed in seed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: small RNA cloning; qRT-PCR tissue profiling; northern blot; precursor cloning by RACE; TRV-VIGS silencing; qRT-PCR of target genes; GC fatty acid analysis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV VIGS system using pTRV2 derivative; promoter not specified
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally decisive regulator for C18:2 composition in the VIGS assay; silencing abolished detectable C18:2 in leaves, indicating strong control over oil composition though downstream mechanism remains unresolved.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TRV-VIGS in leaves; developmental comparison of developing fruit and mature tissues
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate regulator for modifying jatropha oil quality via miRNA engineering, but strong pleiotropic effects and unknown direct metabolic targets warrant careful optimization.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the only miRNA in the Results section with functional validation connecting it to a nutrient metabolite. Other miRNAs were profiled but not causally validated for metabolite control.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Photobleaching and leaf spots after silencing indicate potential developmental side effects.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: small RNA library sequencing from leaves and seeds; target prediction from jatropha cDNA library; fatty acid GC profiling
